schema_version: v0 — migrates when Q6 (intention envelope) lands
owner: akiendelllinux_cc_0 (CC) — CC gates writes; the design is Akien-ratified (signature gate, 2026-07-17 'I think we're done, yes. CC++').
intention: Make the node state-machine physics: the emit-chokepoint enforces the transition table over the state vocabulary (the -ME summons states; the PROVED / LEARNING rests), validates every workflow string against a known versioned node-class definition, and journals every transition and every workflow migration. The design lives in the MAP stone 'The node state-machine — states as summons'; this node is the work to turn that prose into enforcement (Law 4).
why: Today /sorted 'refuses illegal transitions' only as prose in the skill — an IOU. The vocabulary is ratified but not yet enforced; until the chokepoint enforces it, the refusal is policy, not physics. This node is where the vocabulary becomes the physics the whole ticket system runs on (state IS the pipeline instance). Cast self-hosting: the first node whose own `state` is written in the vocabulary it will enforce.
traces_to: Law 1 (state IS the pipeline instance — no re-derivation)
MAP 'The learning loop is the primitive'
state: code-seam@v1: THINKME -> [TICKETME] -> BUILDME -> PROVEME -> LEARNME -> PROVED   (cursor at TICKETME: the design (THINKME) is wrapped + ratified and decomposed into the children below; BUILDME waits on the emit-chokepoint, which is part of the base-class / CP-diagnostic spine step. So this node is cast and decomposing, waiting on the base class — a parent-infrastructure dependency — not buildable yet.)
gates: proof gate (tester) — IOU. The proof a hollow build could not pass: construct a node whose workflow attempts an illegal transition and show the chokepoint refuses it; migrate a workflow by string-edit and show the journal crossing + version-validation; a driver whose method-pointer is unproven is refused (Law 8). Durability gate (code-seam): tested AND committed.
falsifier: the vocabulary can't express a real node's lifecycle, OR a node arises needing a state/transition the vocabulary lacks, OR the -ME grammar rule mis-names a state (a summons with no -ME, or a node's-own-condition carrying one). Wrong-shape signal: if enforcing the transition table makes casting so rigid that real work routes around it, the table is wrong, not the work.
horizon: first real enforcement — when the emit-chokepoint (base-class spine step) lands and the first node's transitions are gated by physics rather than the /sorted skill's prose.
children: (pending) complete the transition table — all legal edges incl. back-edges by severity (PROVEME red -> BUILDME / TICKETME / THINKME; ask-Akien escalation for very-wrong) and the dispositions (DROPPED / SUPERSEDED / RETIRED) fully specified.
(pending) concept-piece workflow finalized (THINKME -> REVIEWME -> PROVED; learns by supersession, not LEARNME).
(pending) the emit-chokepoint that validates workflow strings against node_classes/ versioned definitions and journals transitions + migrations — belongs to the base-class / CP-diagnostic spine step.

(advanced 2026-07-18, cairn 584aa74) ground_loop-as-driver-executor — hypothesis CONFIRMED for the simple sensor case: one GroundLoopDevice.run_driver wires an arbitrary proven method to an arbitrary owned target (proven-space via the method-registry = Law 8, owner-gated target via db_domain = Law 6, run-record = LEARNING not RUNNING). The method-registry (cairn/ground_loop/registry.py) is built: admission gated by the tester (a method enters proven-space only if its proof passes). STILL OPEN: whether one executor also covers transforms with sources, and back-edge routing — measured as more drivers pull those; the emit-chokepoint that validates workflow strings + journals transitions is still unbuilt (this ticket's core, waiting on it).

(advanced 2026-07-18, cairn 5cb593a) BOTH firing modes now real — the scheduler (a SYSTEM SERVICE surfaced by the system_rackmount device, cairn/system_rackmount/services/scheduler.py) fires drivers by declarative trigger: interval/date/quantity (scheduled/batched) and state (reactive — wake on state entry, the -ME summons). tick(now, context) is one waking pulse that drains fired drivers through ground_loop, proving the two 'Peers fire in two modes' as one trigger-evaluation. NOTE the identity correction: the scheduler is NOT the rack — the rack is the chassis (bus + shims); the scheduler is a system service via the system rackmount (device-independent abstraction of host system services). STILL OPEN for the primitive: the harvester + sleep-pass as self-hosting drivers (need the emit-chokepoint's owned mount-table), the OS-specific wall-clock backing that pulses tick on a real cadence (a filed edge on the scheduler), and back-edge routing.

(RESHAPED 2026-07-18 evening — SUPERSEDES the two entries above) A design conversation with Akien converged the runtime model and revealed the ground_loop-executor + scheduler-service code was the WRONG shape. The authoritative model is now `CairnCommons/intentions/I-heartbeat-callbacks-and-bus.md`. Corrections that bear on THIS ticket: (1) the ground_loop is ONLY the HEARTBEAT (one daemon that beats) — it is not an executor; the run_driver logic was a goof. (2) Firing lives in the SHIM, not the ground loop: a device subscribes by a file in its tree, its shim fires the device's due CALLBACKS on each beat and STARTS the device (its own process) on demand. (3) CALLBACK (immutable, stateless, class-space) vs TICKET (mutable WORKFLOW node) are two species — LEARNING is a STATE, a callback is the WORKER a LEARNING node sets. (4) A trigger is ANYTHING THAT EVALUATES TO TRUE — the interval/date/quantity/state enum was a reification; evaluate where the data is OWNED (Law 6). (5) The BUS is the sole comm path (symmetric with db_domain=sole state path). The operational-driver primitive survives but is re-expressed in these terms; this ticket still WAITS ON the emit-chokepoint to enforce the state table.

(REWORK SHIPPED 2026-07-18 evening) The code caught up to the reshaped model the same night, each piece proven under the tester and committed: the BUS (cairn d27ce1d), the Callback primitive + BaseShim's per-pulse firing (ae2d372), the ground_loop stripped to the heartbeat (a535de0), and system_rackmount reworked to the system device (faafb70). Bearing on THIS ticket specifically: (a) the CALLBACK/TICKET boundary is now clean IN CODE — cairn/base/callback.py is the immutable worker species; the mutable TICKET workflow-node species (this ticket) stays distinct and DEFERRED, still waiting on the emit-chokepoint. (b) The proven-space METHOD-REGISTRY (cairn/ground_loop/registry.py, the tested code-admission gate from 584aa74) was RETIRED with the driver-executor — its only consumer was run_driver. Its design was sound and proven; it RETURNS with the emit-chokepoint (this ticket's core), which needs exactly that Law-8 gate to admit the code a workflow-string resolves a method to. So the emit-chokepoint build re-instates the registry rather than re-deriving it (Law 1 — the answered question 'how is a method gated into proven-space?' is already structure, in git history at 584aa74). Still UNBUILT here: the emit-chokepoint that validates workflow strings against versioned node-classes + journals transitions/migrations.

(CROSS-REF 2026-07-21 — this ticket UNCHANGED) The emit-chokepoint's OWNERSHIP resolved in the harbor_master /sorted (tickets/harbor-master.json). It FACTORS into three rungs: RULES (transition legality vs the versioned class table) stay HERE — base-class physics, inherited, inescapable, confirmed; AUTHORITY (who may invoke a transition) is owner-gated + delegable per-operation (Law 6), owned by the harbor_master's CLEARANCE gate; TRUTH (the record) is TWO-VANTAGE — the workflow's own append-only `history` (owned by its intention, tickets/charter-state-history-split.json) plus the harbor_master's AGGREGATE register (the fleet-scale query surface). So this ticket's validation half is affirmed base-class; the record + clearance halves now have a HOME (harbor_master) instead of abstract base-class machinery, without becoming the ground_loop-executor goof (the harbor never sails). The method-registry still RETURNS with the chokepoint, re-instated by the harbor_master's clearance gate (Law 8).
waits_on: the emit-chokepoint (base-class / CP-diagnostic base spine step) — the vocabulary cannot be enforced before the chokepoint that enforces it exists. Design-complete now; buildable when the base class lands.
provenance: cast 2026-07-17 via a hand-run /sorted at the close of the state-vocabulary design session (post-compact). The design converged across the session: horizon-of-awareness -> LEARNING/sleep -> tickets-as-operational-drivers -> workflow-as-mutable-string. Ratified by Akien 'I think we're done, yes. CC++'. Self-hosting: the first node cast in its own vocabulary.
